sessions_sample.raw: six runs that are 15 or more miles long
sessions_sample.parsing_note: Distance is presented as a range without specific values.

sessions_sample.raw: one 16 - 18 miler, one 18, one 18 - 20 miler, one straight - up 20 miler.
sessions_sample.parsing_note: Multiple distances presented in a single sentence, requires extraction of individual values.

sessions_sample.raw: SSSC’s: Super Short Strength Circuits, which we’ll demonstrate on video. By Super Short, we mean < 5 minutes for the whole circuit; we’ll ask you to do SSSC’s three times weekly.
sessions_sample.parsing_note: Strength training session lacks distance and duration, which may not fit existing rules.

sessions_sample.raw: Pick three of each to each session and remember to shake it up from one session to the next.
sessions_sample.parsing_note: Instructional text without clear session structure or specific activity types.

unhandled_patterns.issue: Distance range presented without clear upper or lower bounds for individual sessions.
unhandled_patterns.suggested_rule: Allow for distance ranges in total weekly mileage and specify how to handle them.

unhandled_patterns.pattern: two ways to sneak in more strength training: a fun circuit run once a week and SSSC’s: Super Short Strength Circuits
unhandled_patterns.issue: Multiple training types mentioned in a single sentence without clear separation.
unhandled_patterns.suggested_rule: Recognize and parse multiple training types mentioned in a single context.

new_abbreviations.example: we’ll ask you to do SSSC’s three times weekly.

prompt_improvements: Allow for parsing of distance ranges in total weekly mileage and specify how to handle them.
Recognize and parse multiple training types mentioned in a single context.
anomalies: The plan includes a mix of instructional text and session descriptions, making it difficult to extract structured session data.
